description: Central scenario, seed 0; enhanced FRS 2023-24 with geo_impact.py's SOC imputation (route B). Decomposes the geography-section (+enhanced, 2025) vs main (+plain, 2026) BHC poverty-change gap.

decomposition_bhc_poverty_pp.note: single seed-0 run per cell; imputation noise not separately varied (imputation seed fixed at 0).

exchequer_cost_note.warning: exchequer_cost_bn in the enhanced cells is NOT comparable to the plain-FRS figures.
exchequer_cost_note.diagnosis: The enhanced FRS 2023-24 calibrates capital incomes to administrative totals (baseline weighted dividend income ~GBP 888bn vs a much smaller plain-FRS base). The JR16 capital shock scales dividend/interest income by x1.398, adding ~GBP 353bn of dividends and ~+GBP 130bn income tax, which swamps the ~-GBP 64bn earnings loss (hence gov_balance IMPROVES by ~GBP 119bn). Channel decomposition, seed 0, 2026: delta income_tax +129.9bn, NI -2.6bn, UC +0.1bn, employment_income -64.5bn, dividend_income +353.3bn.
exchequer_cost_note.implication: Use the enhanced cells only for the poverty decomposition (M7); the proportional capital-return scaling should be re-parameterised before fiscal use on this dataset.
